mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware and models. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM CLI tool to manage deployments and configurations.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.17.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a managed Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the deployment configuration.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> This YAML file sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Remove vLLM Deployment
uninstall.description:
This will remove the vLLM deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region us-central1

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get deployment vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

kubectl set image deployment/vllm vllm=<new-image-tag>

kubectl rollout status deployment/vllm

```

> Backup the current deployment configuration.
> Replace <new-image-tag> with the new version of vLLM.
> Verify that the rollout was successful.
commands: kubectl get deployment vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image tag does not exist or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image tag in your deployment YAML and ensure it is correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1' to reset kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
